attacking_style: Breakaway specialist who targets intermediate sprints and stage placements rather than outright wins. Operates from long-range moves but calculates effort expenditure carefully.
team_tactics: Appears to operate with autonomy in breakaways, using team resources minimally. Focuses on individual opportunities rather than coordinated team attacks.
terrain_preference: Stages suited to breakaway success where intermediate sprints offer value. Capable of lasting in breaks over varied terrain but not targeting summit finishes.
vulnerability: Not a pure finisher—prioritizes intermediate points over stage wins. Likely struggles when breaks are controlled tightly or in pure sprint/climbing specialist scenarios.
key_patterns: Takes intermediate sprints from breakaways across multiple Grand Tours (2019, 2021, 2023 confirmed)
Demonstrates calculated energy management: shelters late in breaks, conserves for placement over victory
Targets bonus money/points opportunistically rather than stage wins in Grand Tour context
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 observation shows continuation of established breakaway/sprint pattern without elevation or decline in approach
built_from: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 6
